summary: Whiplash and steering wheel impressions help determine involvement as driver vs. passenger, while sparrows' footmarks don't contribute meaningful differentiation due to their irrelevance to human interactions during accidents. Thus, \(\boxed{\text{sparrows' footmarks}}\) were non-informative for identification purposes.
